332	export class Factory<E extends Env = Env, P extends string = string> {
333	  private initApp?: InitApp<E>
334	  #defaultAppOptions?: HonoOptions<E>
335	
336	  constructor(init?: { initApp?: InitApp<E>; defaultAppOptions?: HonoOptions<E> }) {
337	    this.initApp = init?.initApp
338	    this.#defaultAppOptions = init?.defaultAppOptions
339	  }
340	
341	  createApp = (options?: HonoOptions<E>): Hono<E> => {
342	    const app = new Hono<E>(
343	      options && this.#defaultAppOptions
344	        ? { ...this.#defaultAppOptions, ...options }
345	        : (options ?? this.#defaultAppOptions)
346	    )
347	    if (this.initApp) {
348	      this.initApp(app)
349	    }
350	    return app
351	  }
352	
353	  createMiddleware = <I extends Input = {}, R extends HandlerResponse<any> | void = void>(
354	    middleware: MiddlewareHandler<E, P, I, R extends void ? Response : R>
355	  ): MiddlewareHandler<E, P, I, R extends void ? Response : R> => middleware
356	
357	  createHandlers: CreateHandlersInterface<E, P> = (...handlers: any) => {
358	    // @ts-expect-error this should not be typed
359	    return handlers.filter((handler) => handler !== undefined)
360	  }
361	}
362	
363	export const createFactory = <E extends Env = Env, P extends string = string>(init?: {
364	  initApp?: InitApp<E>
365	  defaultAppOptions?: HonoOptions<E>
366	}): Factory<E, P> => new Factory<E, P>(init)
367	
368	export const createMiddleware = <
369	  E extends Env = any,
370	  P extends string = string,
371	  I extends Input = {},
372	  R extends HandlerResponse<any> | void = void,
373	>(
374	  middleware: MiddlewareHandler<E, P, I, R extends void ? Response : R>
375	): MiddlewareHandler<E, P, I, R extends void ? Response : R> => middleware

/**
 * @module
 * Factory Helper for Hono.
 */

/* eslint-disable @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any */
import { Hono } from '../../hono'
import type { HonoOptions } from '../../hono-base'
import type {
  Env,
  H,
  HandlerResponse,
  Input,
  IntersectNonAnyTypes,
  MiddlewareHandler,
} from '../../types'

type InitApp<E extends Env = Env> = (app: Hono<E>) => void

export interface CreateHandlersInterface<E extends Env, P extends string> {
  <I extends Input = {}, R extends HandlerResponse<any> = any, E2 extends Env = E>(
    handler1: H<E2, P, I, R>
  ): [H<E2, P, I, R>]

  // handler x2
  <
    I extends Input = {},
    I2 extends Input = I,
    R extends HandlerResponse<any> = any,
    R2 extends HandlerResponse<any> = any,
    E2 extends Env = E,
    E3 extends Env = IntersectNonAnyTypes<[E, E2]>,
  >(
    handler1: H<E2, P, I, R>,
    handler2: H<E3, P, I2, R2>
  ): [H<E2, P, I, R>, H<E3, P, I2, R2>]

  // handler x3
  <
    I extends Input = {},
    I2 extends Input = I,
    I3 extends Input = I & I2,
    R extends HandlerResponse<any> = any,
    R2 extends HandlerResponse<any> = any,
    R3 extends HandlerResponse<any> = any,
    E2 extends Env = E,
    E3 extends Env = IntersectNonAnyTypes<[E, E2]>,
    E4 extends Env = IntersectNonAnyTypes<[E, E2, E3]>,
  >(
    handler1: H<E2, P, I, R>,
    handler2: H<E3, P, I2, R2>,
    handler3: H<E4, P, I3, R3>
  ): [H<E2, P, I, R>, H<E3, P, I2, R2>, H<E4, P, I3, R3>]

  // handler x4
  <
    I extends Input = {},
    I2 extends Input = I,
    I3 extends Input = I & I2,
    I4 extends Input = I & I2 & I3,
    R extends HandlerResponse<any> = any,
    R2 extends HandlerResponse<any> = any,
    R3 extends HandlerResponse<any> = any,
    R4 extends HandlerResponse<any> = any,
    E2 extends Env = E,
    E3 extends Env = IntersectNonAnyTypes<[E, E2]>,
    E4 extends Env = IntersectNonAnyTypes<[E, E2, E3]>,
    E5 extends Env = IntersectNonAnyTypes<[E, E2, E3, E4]>,
  >(
    handler1: H<E2, P, I, R>,
    handler2: H<E3, P, I2, R2>,
    handler3: H<E4, P, I3, R3>,
    handler4: H<E5, P, I4, R4>
  ): [H<E2, P, I, R>, H<E3, P, I2, R2>, H<E4, P, I3, R3>, H<E5, P, I4, R4>]

  // handler x5
  <
    I extends Input = {},
    I2 extends Input = I,
    I3 extends Input = I & I2,
    I4 extends Input = I & I2 & I3,
    I5 extends Input = I & I2 & I3 & I4,
    R extends HandlerResponse<any> = any,
    R2 extends HandlerResponse<any> = any,
    R3 extends HandlerResponse<any> = any,
    R4 extends HandlerResponse<any> = any,
    R5 extends HandlerResponse<any> = any,
    E2 extends Env = E,
    E3 extends Env = IntersectNonAnyTypes<[E, E2]>,
    E4 extends Env = IntersectNonAnyTypes<[E, E2, E3]>,
    E5 extends Env = IntersectNonAnyTypes<[E, E2, E3, E4]>,
    E6 extends Env = IntersectNonAnyTypes<[E, E2, E3, E4, E5]>,
  >(
    handler1: H<E2, P, I, R>,
    handler2: H<E3, P, I2, R2>,
    handler3: H<E4, P, I3, R3>,
    handler4: H<E5, P, I4, R4>,
    handler5: H<E6, P, I5, R5>
  ): [H<E2, P, I, R>, H<E3, P, I2, R2>, H<E4, P, I3, R3>, H<E5, P, I4, R4>, H<E6, P, I5, R5>]

  // handler x6
  <
    I extends Input = {},
    I2 extends Input = I,
    I3 extends Input = I & I2,
    I4 extends Input = I & I2 & I3,
    I5 extends Input = I & I2 & I3 & I4,

export class Factory<E extends Env = Env, P extends string = string> {
  private initApp?: InitApp<E>
  #defaultAppOptions?: HonoOptions<E>

  constructor(init?: { initApp?: InitApp<E>; defaultAppOptions?: HonoOptions<E> }) {
    this.initApp = init?.initApp
    this.#defaultAppOptions = init?.defaultAppOptions
  }

  createApp = (options?: HonoOptions<E>): Hono<E> => {
    const app = new Hono<E>(
      options && this.#defaultAppOptions
        ? { ...this.#defaultAppOptions, ...options }
        : (options ?? this.#defaultAppOptions)
    )
    if (this.initApp) {
      this.initApp(app)
    }
    return app
  }

  createMiddleware = <I extends Input = {}, R extends HandlerResponse<any> | void = void>(
    middleware: MiddlewareHandler<E, P, I, R extends void ? Response : R>
  ): MiddlewareHandler<E, P, I, R extends void ? Response : R> => middleware

  createHandlers: CreateHandlersInterface<E, P> = (...handlers: any) => {
    // @ts-expect-error this should not be typed
    return handlers.filter((handler) => handler !== undefined)
  }
}

export const createFactory = <E extends Env = Env, P extends string = string>(init?: {
  initApp?: InitApp<E>
  defaultAppOptions?: HonoOptions<E>
}): Factory<E, P> => new Factory<E, P>(init)

export const createMiddleware = <
  E extends Env = any,
  P extends string = string,
  I extends Input = {},
  R extends HandlerResponse<any> | void = void,
>(
  middleware: MiddlewareHandler<E, P, I, R extends void ? Response : R>
): MiddlewareHandler<E, P, I, R extends void ? Response : R> => middleware

import type { Context } from '../../context'
import { GET_MATCH_RESULT } from '../../request/constants'
import type { RouterRoute } from '../../types'
import { getPattern, splitRoutingPath } from '../../utils/url'

/**
 * Get matched routes in the handler
 *
 * @param {Context} c - The context object
 * @returns An array of matched routes
 *
 * @example
 * ```ts
 * import { matchedRoutes } from 'hono/route'
 *
 * app.use('*', async function logger(c, next) {
 *   await next()
 *   matchedRoutes(c).forEach(({ handler, method, path }, i) => {
 *     const name = handler.name || (handler.length < 2 ? '[handler]' : '[middleware]')
 *     console.log(
 *       method,
 *       ' ',
 *       path,
 *       ' '.repeat(Math.max(10 - path.length, 0)),
 *       name,
 *       i === c.req.routeIndex ? '<- respond from here' : ''
 *     )
 *   })
 * })
 * ```
 */
export const matchedRoutes = (c: Context): RouterRoute[] =>
  // @ts-expect-error c.req[GET_MATCH_RESULT] is not typed
  (c.req as unknown)[GET_MATCH_RESULT][0].map(([[, route]]) => route)

/**
 * Get the route path registered within the handler
 *
 * @param {Context} c - The context object
 * @param {number} index - The index of the root from which to retrieve the path, similar to Array.prototype.at(), where a negative number is the index counted from the end of the matching root. Defaults to the current root index.
 * @returns The route path registered within the handler
 *
 * @example
 * ```ts
 * import { routePath } from 'hono/route'
 *
 * app.use('*', (c, next) => {
 *   console.log(routePath(c)) // '*'
 *   console.log(routePath(c, -1)) // '/posts/:id'
 *   return next()
 * })
 *
 * app.get('/posts/:id', (c) => {
 *   return c.text(routePath(c)) // '/posts/:id'
 * })
 * ```
 */
export const routePath = (c: Context, index?: number): string =>
  matchedRoutes(c).at(index ?? c.req.routeIndex)?.path ?? ''

/**
 * Get the basePath of the as-is route specified by routing.
 *
 * @param {Context} c - The context object
 * @param {number} index - The index of the root from which to retrieve the path, similar to Array.prototype.at(), where a negative number is the index counted from the end of the matching root. Defaults to the current root index.
 * @returns The basePath of the as-is route specified by routing.
 *
 * @example
 * ```ts
 * import { baseRoutePath } from 'hono/route'
 *
 * const app = new Hono()
 *
 * const subApp = new Hono()
 * subApp.get('/posts/:id', (c) => {
 *   return c.text(baseRoutePath(c)) // '/:sub'
 * })
 *
 * app.route('/:sub', subApp)
 * ```
 */
export const baseRoutePath = (c: Context, index?: number): string =>
  matchedRoutes(c).at(index ?? c.req.routeIndex)?.basePath ?? ''

/**
 * Get the basePath with embedded parameters
 *
 * @param {Context} c - The context object
 * @param {number} index - The index of the root from which to retrieve the path, similar to Array.prototype.at(), where a negative number is the index counted from the end of the matching root. Defaults to the current root index.
 * @returns The basePath with embedded parameters.
 *
 * @example
 * ```ts
 * import { basePath } from 'hono/route'
 *
 * const app = new Hono()
 *
 * const subApp = new Hono()
 * subApp.get('/posts/:id', (c) => {
 *   return c.text(basePath(c)) // '/requested-sub-app-path'
 * })
 *
 * app.route('/:sub', subApp)
 * ```
 */
const basePathCacheMap: WeakMap<Context, Record<number, string>> = new WeakMap()
export const basePath = (c: Context, index?: number): string => {
  index ??= c.req.routeIndex

  const cache = basePathCacheMap.get(c) || []
  if (typeof cache[index] === 'string') {
    return cache[index]
  }

  let result: string
  const rp = baseRoutePath(c, index)
  if (!/[:*]/.test(rp)) {
    result = rp
  } else {
    const paths = splitRoutingPath(rp)

    const reqPath = c.req.path
    let basePathLength = 0
    for (let i = 0, len = paths.length; i < len; i++) {
      const pattern = getPattern(paths[i], paths[i + 1])
      if (pattern) {
        const re = pattern[2] === true || pattern === '*' ? /[^\/]+/ : pattern[2]
        basePathLength += reqPath.substring(basePathLength + 1).match(re)?.[0].length || 0
      } else {
        basePathLength += paths[i].length
      }
      basePathLength += 1 // for '/'
    }
    result = reqPath.substring(0, basePathLength)
  }

  cache[index] = result
  basePathCacheMap.set(c, cache)

  return result
}

40	}
41	
42	/**
43	 * ReadyState for WebSocket
44	 */
45	export type WSReadyState = 0 | 1 | 2 | 3
46	
47	/**
48	 * An argument for WSContext class
49	 */
50	export interface WSContextInit<T = unknown> {
51	  send(data: string | ArrayBuffer | Uint8Array, options: SendOptions): void
52	  close(code?: number, reason?: string): void
53	
54	  raw?: T
55	  readyState: WSReadyState
56	  url?: string | URL | null
57	  protocol?: string | null
58	}
59	
60	/**
61	 * Options for sending message
62	 */
63	export interface SendOptions {
64	  compress?: boolean
65	}
66	
67	/**
68	 * A context for controlling WebSockets
69	 */
70	export class WSContext<T = unknown> {
71	  #init: WSContextInit<T>
72	  constructor(init: WSContextInit<T>) {
73	    this.#init = init
74	    this.raw = init.raw
75	    this.url = init.url ? new URL(init.url) : null
76	    this.protocol = init.protocol ?? null
77	  }
78	  send(source: string | ArrayBuffer | Uint8Array<ArrayBuffer>, options?: SendOptions): void {
79	    this.#init.send(source, options ?? {})
80	  }
81	  raw?: T
82	  binaryType: BinaryType = 'arraybuffer'
83	  get readyState(): WSReadyState {
84	    return this.#init.readyState
85	  }
86	  url: URL | null
87	  protocol: string | null
88	  close(code?: number, reason?: string) {
89	    this.#init.close(code, reason)
90	  }
91	}
92	
93	export type WSMessageReceive = string | Blob | ArrayBufferLike
94	
95	export const createWSMessageEvent = (source: WSMessageReceive): MessageEvent<WSMessageReceive> => {
96	  return new MessageEvent<WSMessageReceive>('message', {
97	    data: source,
98	  })
99	}
100	
101	export interface WebSocketHelperDefineContext {}
102	export type WebSocketHelperDefineHandler<T, U> = (
103	  c: Context,
104	  events: WSEvents<T>,
105	  options?: U
106	) => Promise<Response | void> | Response | void
107	
108	/**
109	 * Create a WebSocket adapter/helper
110	 */
111	export const defineWebSocketHelper = <T = unknown, U = any>(
112	  handler: WebSocketHelperDefineHandler<T, U>
113	): UpgradeWebSocket<T, U> => {
114	  return ((
115	    ...args:
116	      | [createEvents: (c: Context) => WSEvents<T> | Promise<WSEvents<T>>, options?: U]
117	      | [c: Context, events: WSEvents<T>, options?: U]
118	  ) => {
119	    if (typeof args[0] === 'function') {
120	      const [createEvents, options] = args
121	      return async function upgradeWebSocket(c, next) {
122	        const events = await createEvents(c)
123	        const result = await handler(c, events, options as U)
124	        if (result) {
125	          return result
126	        }
127	        await next()
128	      }
129	    } else {
130	      const [c, events, options] = args as [c: Context, events: WSEvents<T>, options?: U]
131	      return (async () => {
132	        const upgraded = await handler(c, events, options as U)
133	        if (!upgraded) {
134	          throw new Error('Failed to upgrade WebSocket')
135	        }
136	        return upgraded
137	      })()
138	    }
139	  }) as UpgradeWebSocket<T, U>
140	}

1	/**
2	 * @module
3	 * Adapter Helper for Hono.
4	 */
5	
6	import type { Context } from '../../context'
7	
8	export type Runtime = 'node' | 'deno' | 'bun' | 'workerd' | 'fastly' | 'edge-light' | 'other'
9	
10	export const env = <
11	  T extends Record<string, unknown>,
12	  C extends Context = Context<{
13	    Bindings: T
14	  }>,
15	>(
16	  c: T extends Record<string, unknown> ? Context : C,
17	  runtime?: Runtime
18	): T & C['env'] => {
19	  // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
20	  const global = globalThis as any
21	  const globalEnv = global?.process?.env as T
22	
23	  runtime ??= getRuntimeKey()
24	
25	  const runtimeEnvHandlers: Record<string, () => T> = {
26	    bun: () => globalEnv,
27	    node: () => globalEnv,
28	    'edge-light': () => globalEnv,
29	    deno: () => {
30	      // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/ban-ts-comment
31	      // @ts-ignore
32	      return Deno.env.toObject() as T
33	    },
34	    workerd: () => c.env,
35	    // On Fastly Compute, you can use the ConfigStore to manage user-defined data.
36	    fastly: () => ({}) as T,
37	    other: () => ({}) as T,
38	  }
39	
40	  return runtimeEnvHandlers[runtime]()
41	}
42	
43	export const knownUserAgents: Partial<Record<Runtime, string>> = {
44	  deno: 'Deno',
45	  bun: 'Bun',
46	  workerd: 'Cloudflare-Workers',
47	  node: 'Node.js',
48	}
49	
50	export const getRuntimeKey = (): Runtime => {
51	  // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
52	  const global = globalThis as any
53	
54	  // check if the current runtime supports navigator.userAgent
55	  const userAgentSupported =
56	    typeof navigator !== 'undefined' && typeof navigator.userAgent === 'string'
57	
58	  // if supported, check the user agent
59	  if (userAgentSupported) {
60	    for (const [runtimeKey, userAgent] of Object.entries(knownUserAgents)) {
61	      if (checkUserAgentEquals(userAgent)) {
62	        return runtimeKey as Runtime
63	      }
64	    }
65	  }
66	
67	  // check if running on Edge Runtime
68	  if (typeof global?.EdgeRuntime === 'string') {
69	    return 'edge-light'
70	  }
71	
72	  // check if running on Fastly
73	  if (global?.fastly !== undefined) {
74	    return 'fastly'
75	  }
76	
77	  // userAgent isn't supported before Node v21.1.0; so fallback to the old way
78	  if (global?.process?.release?.name === 'node') {
79	    return 'node'
80	  }
81	
82	  // couldn't detect the runtime
83	  return 'other'
84	}
85	
86	export const checkUserAgentEquals = (platform: string): boolean => {
87	  const userAgent = navigator.userAgent
88	
89	  return userAgent.startsWith(platform)
90	}

14	  OnInvalidSlug,
15	} from './common'

18	  DEFAULT_STYLE_ID,
19	  PSEUDO_GLOBAL_SELECTOR,
20	  SELECTOR,

22	  STYLE_STRING,

24	  cxCommon,
25	  keyframesCommon,
26	  viewTransitionCommon,
27	} from './common'

29	export type { ClassNameSlug, OnInvalidSlug } from './common'
30	
31	type CssClassName = HtmlEscapedString & CssClassNameCommon
32	
33	type usedClassNameData = [
34	  Record<string, string>, // class name to add
35	  Record<string, true>, // class name already added
36	]
37	
38	interface CssType {
39	  (strings: TemplateStringsArray, ...values: CssVariableType[]): Promise<string>
40	}
41	
42	interface CxType {
43	  (
44	    ...args: (CssClassName | Promise<string> | string | boolean | null | undefined)[]
45	  ): Promise<string>

49	  (strings: TemplateStringsArray, ...values: CssVariableType[]): CssClassNameCommon

62	/**
63	 * @experimental
64	 * `createCssContext` is an experimental feature.
65	 * The API might be changed.
66	 *
67	 * @param options.id - The ID for the style element
68	 * @param options.classNameSlug - Optional function to customize generated CSS class names
69	 * @param options.onInvalidSlug - Optional callback function called when an invalid slug is returned from ClassNameSlug
70	 */
71	export const createCssContext = ({
72	  id,
73	  classNameSlug,
74	  onInvalidSlug,
75	}: {
76	  id: Readonly<string>
77	  classNameSlug?: ClassNameSlug
78	  onInvalidSlug?: OnInvalidSlug
79	}): DefaultContextType => {
80	  const [cssJsxDomObject, StyleRenderToDom] = createCssJsxDomObjects({ id })
81	
82	  const contextMap: WeakMap<object, usedClassNameData> = new WeakMap()
83	  const nonceMap: WeakMap<object, string | undefined> = new WeakMap()
84	
85	  const replaceStyleRe = new RegExp(`(<style id="${id}"(?: nonce="[^"]*")?>.*?)(</style>)`)
86	
87	  const newCssClassNameObject = (cssClassName: CssClassNameCommon): Promise<string> => {
88	    const appendStyle: HtmlEscapedCallback = ({ buffer, context }): Promise<string> | undefined => {
89	      const [toAdd, added] = contextMap.get(context) as usedClassNameData
90	      const names = Object.keys(toAdd)
91	
92	      if (!names.length) {
93	        return
94	      }
95	
96	      let stylesStr = ''
97	      names.forEach((className) => {
98	        added[className] = true
99	        stylesStr += className.startsWith(PSEUDO_GLOBAL_SELECTOR)
100	          ? toAdd[className]
101	          : `${className[0] === '@' ? '' : '.'}${className}{${toAdd[className]}}`
102	      })
103	      contextMap.set(context, [{}, added])
104	
105	      if (buffer && replaceStyleRe.test(buffer[0])) {
106	        buffer[0] = buffer[0].replace(replaceStyleRe, (_, pre, post) => `${pre}${stylesStr}${post}`)
107	        return
108	      }
109	
110	      const nonce = nonceMap.get(context)
111	      const appendStyleScript = `<script${
112	        nonce ? ` nonce="${nonce}"` : ''
113	      }>document.querySelector('#${id}').textContent+=${JSON.stringify(stylesStr)}</script>`
114	
115	      if (buffer) {
116	        buffer[0] = `${appendStyleScript}${buffer[0]}`
117	        return
118	      }
119	
120	      return Promise.resolve(appendStyleScript)
121	    }
122	
123	    const addClassNameToContext: HtmlEscapedCallback = ({ context }) => {
124	      if (!contextMap.has(context)) {
125	        contextMap.set(context, [{}, {}])
126	      }
127	      const [toAdd, added] = contextMap.get(context) as usedClassNameData
128	      let allAdded = true
129	      if (!added[cssClassName[SELECTOR]]) {
130	        allAdded = false
131	        toAdd[cssClassName[SELECTOR]] = cssClassName[STYLE_STRING]
132	      }
133	      cssClassName[SELECTORS].forEach(
134	        ({ [CLASS_NAME]: className, [STYLE_STRING]: styleString }) => {
135	          if (!added[className]) {
136	            allAdded = false
137	            toAdd[className] = styleString
138	          }
139	        }
140	      )
141	      if (allAdded) {
142	        return
143	      }
144	
145	      return Promise.resolve(raw('', [appendStyle]))
146	    }
147	
148	    const className = new String(cssClassName[CLASS_NAME]) as CssClassName
149	    Object.assign(className, cssClassName)
150	    ;(className as HtmlEscapedString).isEscaped = true
151	    ;(className as HtmlEscapedString).callbacks = [addClassNameToContext]
152	    const promise = Promise.resolve(className)
153	    Object.assign(promise, cssClassName)
154	
155	    promise.toString = cssJsxDomObject.toString
156	    return promise
157	  }
158	
159	  const css: CssType = (strings, ...values) => {
160	    return newCssClassNameObject(cssCommon(strings, values, classNameSlug, onInvalidSlug))
161	  }
162	
163	  const cx: CxType = (...args) => {
164	    // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
165	    args = cxCommon(args as any) as any
166	    // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
167	    return css(Array(args.length).fill('') as any, ...args)
168	  }
169	
170	  const keyframes: KeyframesType = (strings, ...values) =>
171	    keyframesCommon(strings, values, classNameSlug, onInvalidSlug)
172	
173	  const viewTransition: ViewTransitionType = ((
174	    strings: TemplateStringsArray | Promise<string> | undefined,
175	    ...values: CssVariableType[]
176	  ) => {
177	    return newCssClassNameObject(
178	      viewTransitionCommon(strings as any, values, classNameSlug, onInvalidSlug) // eslint-disable-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
179	    )
180	  }) as ViewTransitionType
181	
182	  const Style: StyleType = ({ children, nonce } = {}) =>
183	    raw(
184	      `<style id="${id}"${nonce ? ` nonce="${nonce}"` : ''}>${
185	        children ? (children as unknown as CssClassName)[STYLE_STRING] : ''
186	      }</style>`,
187	      [
188	        ({ context }) => {
189	          nonceMap.set(context, nonce)
190	          return undefined
191	        },
192	      ]
193	    )
194	
195	  // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
196	  ;(Style as any)[DOM_RENDERER] = StyleRenderToDom
197	
198	  return {
199	    css,
200	    cx,
201	    keyframes,
202	    viewTransition: viewTransition as ViewTransitionType,
203	    Style,
204	  }
205	}
206	
207	interface DefaultContextType {
208	  css: CssType
209	  cx: CxType
210	  keyframes: KeyframesType
211	  viewTransition: ViewTransitionType
212	  Style: StyleType
213	}
214	
215	const defaultContext: DefaultContextType = createCssContext({
216	  id: DEFAULT_STYLE_ID,
217	})

4	export const DEFAULT_STYLE_ID = 'hono-css'

6	export const SELECTOR: unique symbol = Symbol()
7	export const CLASS_NAME: unique symbol = Symbol()
8	export const STYLE_STRING: unique symbol = Symbol()
9	export const SELECTORS: unique symbol = Symbol()
10	export const EXTERNAL_CLASS_NAMES: unique symbol = Symbol()
11	const CSS_ESCAPED: unique symbol = Symbol()
12	
13	export interface CssClassName {
14	  [SELECTOR]: string
15	  [CLASS_NAME]: string
16	  [STYLE_STRING]: string
17	  [SELECTORS]: CssClassName[]
18	  [EXTERNAL_CLASS_NAMES]: string[]
19	}

23	interface CssEscapedString {
24	  [CSS_ESCAPED]: string

27	/**

38	/**
39	 * Used the goober'code as a reference:
40	 * https://github.com/cristianbote/goober/blob/master/src/core/to-hash.js
41	 * MIT License, Copyright (c) 2019 Cristian Bote
42	 */
43	const toHash = (str: string): string => {
44	  let i = 0,
45	    out = 11
46	  while (i < str.length) {
47	    out = (101 * out + str.charCodeAt(i++)) >>> 0
48	  }
49	  return 'css-' + out
50	}
51	
52	const normalizeLabel = (label: string): string => {
53	  return label.trim().replace(/\s+/g, '-')
54	}
55	
56	const isValidClassName = (name: string): boolean => /^-?[_a-zA-Z][_a-zA-Z0-9-]*$/.test(name)
57	
58	// CSS-wide keywords that are invalid as @keyframes names per the spec
59	const RESERVED_KEYFRAME_NAMES = new Set([
60	  'default',
61	  'inherit',
62	  'initial',
63	  'none',
64	  'revert',
65	  'revert-layer',
66	  'unset',
67	])
68	const isValidKeyframeName = (name: string): boolean =>
69	  isValidClassName(name) && !RESERVED_KEYFRAME_NAMES.has(name.toLowerCase())
70	
71	const defaultOnInvalidSlug = (slug: string) => {
72	  console.warn(`Invalid slug: ${slug}`)
73	}
74	
75	const cssStringReStr: string = [
76	  '"(?:(?:\\\\[\\s\\S]|[^"\\\\])*)"', // double quoted string
77	
78	  "'(?:(?:\\\\[\\s\\S]|[^'\\\\])*)'", // single quoted string
79	].join('|')
80	const minifyCssRe: RegExp = new RegExp(
81	  [
82	    '(' + cssStringReStr + ')', // $1: quoted string
83	
84	    '(?:' +
85	      [
86	        '^\\s+', // head whitespace
87	        '\\/\\*.*?\\*\\/\\s*', // multi-line comment
88	        '\\/\\/.*\\n\\s*', // single-line comment
89	        '\\s+$', // tail whitespace
90	      ].join('|') +
91	      ')',
92	
93	    '\\s*;\\s*(}|$)\\s*', // $2: trailing semicolon
94	    '\\s*([{};:,])\\s*', // $3: whitespace around { } : , ;
95	    '(\\s)\\s+', // $4: 2+ spaces
96	  ].join('|'),
97	  'g'
98	)

100	export const minify = (css: string): string => {
101	  return css.replace(minifyCssRe, (_, $1, $2, $3, $4) => $1 || $2 || $3 || $4 || '')

103	
104	type CssVariableBasicType =
105	  | CssClassName
106	  | CssEscapedString
107	  | string
108	  | number
109	  | boolean
110	  | null
111	  | undefined
112	type CssVariableAsyncType = Promise<CssVariableBasicType>
113	type CssVariableArrayType = (CssVariableBasicType | CssVariableAsyncType)[]
114	export type CssVariableType = CssVariableBasicType | CssVariableAsyncType | CssVariableArrayType
115	
116	/**
117	 * A function that customizes generated CSS class names.
118	 *
119	 * @param hash - The default hash-based class name (e.g. `css-1234567890`)
120	 * @param label - The comment label extracted from the CSS template, may be empty.
121	 *   Whitespace is trimmed and inner spaces are replaced with hyphens.
122	 * @param styleString - The minified CSS style string
123	 * @returns The custom class name to use. Must be a safe CSS identifier;
124	 *   otherwise, the default hash is used as a fallback.
125	 */
126	export type ClassNameSlug = (hash: string, label: string, styleString: string) => string
127	
128	/**
129	 * A callback function called when an invalid slug is returned from ClassNameSlug.
130	 *
131	 * @param slug - The invalid slug
132	 */
133	export type OnInvalidSlug = (slug: string) => void
134	
135	export const buildStyleString = (
136	  strings: TemplateStringsArray,
137	  values: CssVariableType[]
138	): [string, string, CssClassName[], string[]] => {
139	  const selectors: CssClassName[] = []
140	  const externalClassNames: string[] = []
141	
142	  const label = strings[0].match(/^\s*\/\*(.*?)\*\//)?.[1] || ''
143	  let styleString = ''
144	  for (let i = 0, len = strings.length; i < len; i++) {
145	    styleString += strings[i]
146	    let vArray = values[i]
147	    if (typeof vArray === 'boolean' || vArray === null || vArray === undefined) {
148	      continue
149	    }
150	
151	    if (!Array.isArray(vArray)) {
152	      vArray = [vArray]
153	    }
154	    for (let j = 0, len = vArray.length; j < len; j++) {
155	      let value = vArray[j]
156	      if (typeof value === 'boolean' || value === null || value === undefined) {
157	        continue
158	      }
159	      if (typeof value === 'string') {
160	        if (/([\\"'\/])/.test(value)) {
161	          styleString += value.replace(/([\\"']|(?<=<)\/)/g, '\\$1')
162	        } else {
163	          styleString += value
164	        }
165	      } else if (typeof value === 'number') {
166	        styleString += value
167	      } else if ((value as CssEscapedString)[CSS_ESCAPED]) {
168	        styleString += (value as CssEscapedString)[CSS_ESCAPED]
169	      } else if ((value as CssClassName)[CLASS_NAME].startsWith('@keyframes ')) {
170	        selectors.push(value as CssClassName)
171	        styleString += ` ${(value as CssClassName)[CLASS_NAME].substring(11)} `
172	      } else {
173	        if (strings[i + 1]?.match(/^\s*{/)) {
174	          // assume this value is a class name
175	          selectors.push(value as CssClassName)
176	          value = `.${(value as CssClassName)[CLASS_NAME]}`
177	        } else {
178	          selectors.push(...(value as CssClassName)[SELECTORS])
179	          externalClassNames.push(...(value as CssClassName)[EXTERNAL_CLASS_NAMES])
180	          value = (value as CssClassName)[STYLE_STRING]
181	          const valueLen = value.length
182	          if (valueLen > 0) {
183	            const lastChar = value[valueLen - 1]
184	            if (lastChar !== ';' && lastChar !== '}') {
185	              value += ';'
186	            }
187	          }
188	        }
189	        styleString += `${value || ''}`
190	      }
191	    }
192	  }
193	
194	  return [label, minify(styleString), selectors, externalClassNames]
195	}
196	
197	export const cssCommon = (
198	  strings: TemplateStringsArray,
199	  values: CssVariableType[],
200	  classNameSlug?: ClassNameSlug,
201	  onInvalidSlug?: OnInvalidSlug
202	): CssClassName => {
203	  let [label, thisStyleString, selectors, externalClassNames] = buildStyleString(strings, values)
204	  const isPseudoGlobal = isPseudoGlobalSelectorRe.exec(thisStyleString)
205	  if (isPseudoGlobal) {
206	    thisStyleString = isPseudoGlobal[1]
207	  }
208	  const hash = toHash(label + thisStyleString)
209	
210	  let customSlug: string | undefined
211	  if (classNameSlug) {
212	    const slug = classNameSlug(hash, normalizeLabel(label), thisStyleString)
213	    if (slug) {
214	      if (isValidClassName(slug)) {
215	        customSlug = slug
216	      } else {
217	        ;(onInvalidSlug || defaultOnInvalidSlug)(slug)
218	      }
219	    }
220	  }
221	
222	  const selector = (isPseudoGlobal ? PSEUDO_GLOBAL_SELECTOR : '') + (customSlug || hash)
223	  const className = (
224	    isPseudoGlobal ? selectors.map((s) => s[CLASS_NAME]) : [selector, ...externalClassNames]
225	  ).join(' ')
226	
227	  return {
228	    [SELECTOR]: selector,
229	    [CLASS_NAME]: className,
230	    [STYLE_STRING]: thisStyleString,
231	    [SELECTORS]: selectors,
232	    [EXTERNAL_CLASS_NAMES]: externalClassNames,
233	  }
234	}
235	
236	export const cxCommon = (
237	  args: (string | boolean | null | undefined | CssClassName)[]
238	): (string | boolean | null | undefined | CssClassName)[] => {
239	  for (let i = 0, len = args.length; i < len; i++) {
240	    const arg = args[i]
241	    if (typeof arg === 'string') {
242	      args[i] = {
243	        [SELECTOR]: '',
244	        [CLASS_NAME]: '',
245	        [STYLE_STRING]: '',
246	        [SELECTORS]: [],
247	        [EXTERNAL_CLASS_NAMES]: [arg],
248	      }
249	    }
250	  }
251	
252	  return args
253	}
254	
255	export const keyframesCommon = (
256	  strings: TemplateStringsArray,
257	  values: CssVariableType[],
258	  classNameSlug?: ClassNameSlug,
259	  onInvalidSlug?: OnInvalidSlug
260	): CssClassName => {
261	  const [label, styleString] = buildStyleString(strings, values)
262	  const hash = toHash(label + styleString)
263	
264	  let customSlug: string | undefined
265	  if (classNameSlug) {
266	    const slug = classNameSlug(hash, normalizeLabel(label), styleString)
267	    if (slug) {
268	      if (isValidKeyframeName(slug)) {
269	        customSlug = slug
270	      } else {
271	        ;(onInvalidSlug || defaultOnInvalidSlug)(slug)
272	      }
273	    }
274	  }
275	
276	  return {
277	    [SELECTOR]: '',
278	    [CLASS_NAME]: `@keyframes ${customSlug || hash}`,
279	    [STYLE_STRING]: styleString,
280	    [SELECTORS]: [],
281	    [EXTERNAL_CLASS_NAMES]: [],
282	  }
283	}
284	
285	type ViewTransitionType = {
286	  (
287	    strings: TemplateStringsArray,
288	    values: CssVariableType[],
289	    classNameSlug?: ClassNameSlug,
290	    onInvalidSlug?: OnInvalidSlug
291	  ): CssClassName
292	  (content: CssClassName): CssClassName
293	  (): CssClassName
294	}
295	
296	let viewTransitionNameIndex = 0
297	export const viewTransitionCommon: ViewTransitionType = ((
298	  strings: TemplateStringsArray | CssClassName | undefined,
299	  values: CssVariableType[],
300	  classNameSlug?: ClassNameSlug,
301	  onInvalidSlug?: OnInvalidSlug
302	): CssClassName => {
303	  if (!strings) {
304	    // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
305	    strings = [`/* h-v-t ${viewTransitionNameIndex++} */`] as any
306	  }
307	  const content = Array.isArray(strings)
308	    ? cssCommon(strings as TemplateStringsArray, values, classNameSlug, onInvalidSlug)
309	    : (strings as CssClassName)
310	
311	  const transitionName = content[CLASS_NAME]
312	  const res = cssCommon(
313	    ['view-transition-name:', ''] as any, // eslint-disable-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
314	    [transitionName],
315	    classNameSlug,
316	    onInvalidSlug
317	  )
318	
319	  content[CLASS_NAME] = PSEUDO_GLOBAL_SELECTOR + content[CLASS_NAME]
320	  content[STYLE_STRING] = content[STYLE_STRING].replace(
321	    /(?<=::view-transition(?:[a-z-]*)\()(?=\))/g,
322	    transitionName
323	  )
324	  res[CLASS_NAME] = res[SELECTOR] = transitionName
325	  res[SELECTORS] = [...content[SELECTORS], content]
326	
327	  return res
328	}) as ViewTransitionType

1	/**
2	 * @module
3	 * Proxy Helper for Hono.
4	 */
5	
6	import { HTTPException } from '../../http-exception'
7	import type { RequestHeader } from '../../utils/headers'
8	
9	// https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc2616#section-13.5.1
10	const hopByHopHeaders = [
11	  'connection',
12	  'keep-alive',
13	  'proxy-authenticate',
14	  'proxy-authorization',
15	  'te',
16	  'trailer',
17	  'transfer-encoding',
18	  'upgrade',
19	]
20	
21	// https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc9110#section-5.6.2
22	const ALLOWED_TOKEN_PATTERN = /^[!#$%&'*+\-.0-9A-Z^_`a-z|~]+$/
23	
24	interface ProxyRequestInit extends Omit<RequestInit, 'headers'> {
25	  raw?: Request
26	  headers?:
27	    | HeadersInit
28	    | [string, string][]
29	    | Record<RequestHeader, string | undefined>
30	    | Record<string, string | undefined>
31	  customFetch?: (request: Request) => Promise<Response>
32	  /**
33	   * Enable strict RFC 9110 compliance for Connection header processing.
34	   *
35	   * - `false` (default): Ignores Connection header to prevent potential
36	   *   Hop-by-Hop Header Injection attacks. Recommended for untrusted clients.
37	   * - `true`: Processes Connection header per RFC 9110 and removes listed headers.
38	   *   Only use in trusted environments.
39	   *
40	   * @default false
41	   * @see https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc9110#section-7.6.1
42	   */
43	  strictConnectionProcessing?: boolean
44	}
45	
46	interface ProxyFetch {
47	  (input: string | URL | Request, init?: ProxyRequestInit): Promise<Response>
48	}
49	
50	const buildRequestInitFromRequest = (
51	  request: Request | undefined,
52	  strictConnectionProcessing: boolean
53	): RequestInit & { duplex?: 'half' } => {
54	  if (!request) {
55	    return {}
56	  }
57	
58	  const headers = new Headers(request.headers)
59	
60	  if (strictConnectionProcessing) {
61	    // https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc9110#section-7.6.1
62	    // Parse Connection header and remove listed headers (MUST per RFC 9110)
63	    const connectionValue = headers.get('connection')
64	    if (connectionValue) {
65	      const headerNames = connectionValue.split(',').map((h) => h.trim())
66	      // Validate header names per RFC 9110 Section 5.6.2 (token syntax)
67	      const invalidHeaders = headerNames.filter((h) => !ALLOWED_TOKEN_PATTERN.test(h))
68	
69	      if (invalidHeaders.length > 0) {
70	        throw new HTTPException(400, {
71	          message: `Invalid Connection header value: ${invalidHeaders.join(', ')}`,
72	        })
73	      }
74	      headerNames.forEach((headerName) => {
75	        headers.delete(headerName)
76	      })
77	    }
78	  }
79	
80	  hopByHopHeaders.forEach((header) => {
81	    headers.delete(header)
82	  })
83	
84	  return {
85	    method: request.method,
86	    body: request.body,
87	    duplex: request.body ? 'half' : undefined,
88	    headers,
89	    signal: request.signal,
90	  }
91	}
92	
93	const preprocessRequestInit = (requestInit: RequestInit): RequestInit => {
94	  if (
95	    !requestInit.headers ||
96	    Array.isArray(requestInit.headers) ||
97	    requestInit.headers instanceof Headers
98	  ) {
99	    return requestInit
100	  }
101	
102	  const headers = new Headers()
103	  for (const [key, value] of Object.entries(requestInit.headers)) {
104	    if (value == null) {
105	      // delete header if value is null or undefined
106	      headers.delete(key)
107	    } else {
108	      headers.set(key, value)
109	    }
110	  }
111	  requestInit.headers = headers
112	  return requestInit
113	}
114	
115	/**
116	 * Fetch API wrapper for proxy.
117	 * The parameters and return value are the same as for `fetch` (except for the proxy-specific options).
118	 *
119	 * The “Accept-Encoding” header is replaced with an encoding that the current runtime can handle.
120	 * Unnecessary response headers are deleted and a Response object is returned that can be returned
121	 * as is as a response from the handler.
122	 *
123	 * @example
124	 * ```ts
125	 * app.get('/proxy/:path', (c) => {
126	 *   return proxy(`http://${originServer}/${c.req.param('path')}`, {
127	 *     headers: {
128	 *       ...c.req.header(), // optional, specify only when forwarding all the request data (including credentials) is necessary.
129	 *       'X-Forwarded-For': '127.0.0.1',
130	 *       'X-Forwarded-Host': c.req.header('host'),
131	 *       Authorization: undefined, // do not propagate request headers contained in c.req.header('Authorization')
132	 *     },
133	 *   }).then((res) => {
134	 *     res.headers.delete('Set-Cookie')
135	 *     return res
136	 *   })
137	 * })
138	 *
139	 * app.all('/proxy/:path', (c) => {
140	 *   return proxy(`http://${originServer}/${c.req.param('path')}`, {
141	 *     ...c.req, // optional, specify only when forwarding all the request data (including credentials) is necessary.
142	 *     headers: {
143	 *       ...c.req.header(),
144	 *       'X-Forwarded-For': '127.0.0.1',
145	 *       'X-Forwarded-Host': c.req.header('host'),
146	 *       Authorization: undefined, // do not propagate request headers contained in c.req.header('Authorization')
147	 *     },
148	 *   })
149	 * })
150	 *
151	 * // Strict RFC compliance mode (use only in trusted environments)
152	 * app.get('/internal-proxy/:path', (c) => {
153	 *   return proxy(`http://${internalServer}/${c.req.param('path')}`, {
154	 *     ...c.req,
155	 *     strictConnectionProcessing: true,
156	 *   })
157	 * })
158	 * ```
159	 */
160	export const proxy: ProxyFetch = async (input, proxyInit) => {
161	  const {
162	    raw,
163	    customFetch,
164	    strictConnectionProcessing = false,
165	    ...requestInit
166	  } = proxyInit instanceof Request ? { raw: proxyInit } : (proxyInit ?? {})
167	
168	  const req = new Request(input, {
169	    ...buildRequestInitFromRequest(raw, strictConnectionProcessing),
170	    ...preprocessRequestInit(requestInit as RequestInit),
171	  })
172	  req.headers.delete('accept-encoding')
173	
174	  const res = await (customFetch || fetch)(req)
175	  const resHeaders = new Headers(res.headers)
176	  hopByHopHeaders.forEach((header) => {
177	    resHeaders.delete(header)
178	  })
179	  if (resHeaders.has('content-encoding')) {
180	    resHeaders.delete('content-encoding')
181	    // Content-Length is the size of the compressed content, not the size of the original content
182	    resHeaders.delete('content-length')
183	  }
184	
185	  return new Response(res.body, {
186	    status: res.status,
187	    statusText: res.statusText,
188	    headers: resHeaders,
189	  })
190	}

1	import type { Context } from '../../context'
2	import { HtmlEscapedCallbackPhase, resolveCallback } from '../../utils/html'
3	import { StreamingApi } from '../../utils/stream'
4	import { isOldBunVersion } from './utils'
5	
6	export interface SSEMessage {
7	  data: string | Promise<string>
8	  event?: string
9	  id?: string
10	  retry?: number
11	}
12	
13	export class SSEStreamingApi extends StreamingApi {
14	  constructor(writable: WritableStream, readable: ReadableStream) {
15	    super(writable, readable)
16	  }
17	
18	  async writeSSE(message: SSEMessage) {
19	    const data = await resolveCallback(message.data, HtmlEscapedCallbackPhase.Stringify, false, {})
20	    const dataLines = (data as string)
21	      .split(/\r\n|\r|\n/)
22	      .map((line) => {
23	        return `data: ${line}`
24	      })
25	      .join('\n')
26	
27	    for (const key of ['event', 'id', 'retry'] as (keyof SSEMessage)[]) {
28	      if (message[key] && /[\r\n]/.test(message[key] as string)) {
29	        throw new Error(`${key} must not contain "\\r" or "\\n"`)
30	      }
31	    }
32	
33	    const sseData =
34	      [
35	        message.event && `event: ${message.event}`,
36	        dataLines,
37	        message.id && `id: ${message.id}`,
38	        message.retry && `retry: ${message.retry}`,
39	      ]
40	        .filter(Boolean)
41	        .join('\n') + '\n\n'
42	
43	    await this.write(sseData)
44	  }
45	}
46	
47	const run = async (
48	  stream: SSEStreamingApi,
49	  cb: (stream: SSEStreamingApi) => Promise<void>,
50	  onError?: (e: Error, stream: SSEStreamingApi) => Promise<void>
51	): Promise<void> => {
52	  try {
53	    await cb(stream)
54	  } catch (e) {
55	    if (e instanceof Error && onError) {
56	      await onError(e, stream)
57	
58	      await stream.writeSSE({
59	        event: 'error',
60	        data: e.message,
61	      })
62	    } else {
63	      console.error(e)
64	    }
65	  } finally {
66	    stream.close()
67	  }
68	}
69	
70	const contextStash: WeakMap<ReadableStream, Context> = new WeakMap<ReadableStream, Context>()
71	
72	export const streamSSE = (
73	  c: Context,
74	  cb: (stream: SSEStreamingApi) => Promise<void>,
75	  onError?: (e: Error, stream: SSEStreamingApi) => Promise<void>
76	): Response => {
77	  const { readable, writable } = new TransformStream()
78	  const stream = new SSEStreamingApi(writable, readable)
79	
80	  // Until Bun v1.1.27, Bun didn't call cancel() on the ReadableStream for Response objects from Bun.serve()
81	  if (isOldBunVersion()) {
82	    c.req.raw.signal.addEventListener('abort', () => {
83	      if (!stream.closed) {
84	        stream.abort()
85	      }
86	    })
87	  }
88	
89	  // in bun, `c` is destroyed when the request is returned, so hold it until the end of streaming
90	  contextStash.set(stream.responseReadable, c)
91	
92	  c.header('Transfer-Encoding', 'chunked')
93	  c.header('Content-Type', 'text/event-stream')
94	  c.header('Cache-Control', 'no-cache')
95	  c.header('Connection', 'keep-alive')
96	
97	  run(stream, cb, onError)
98	
99	  return c.newResponse(stream.responseReadable)
100	}
101

1	import type { Context } from '../../context'
2	import { parseAccept } from '../../utils/accept'
3	import type { AcceptHeader } from '../../utils/headers'
4	
5	export interface Accept {
6	  type: string
7	  params: Record<string, string>
8	  q: number
9	}
10	
11	export interface acceptsConfig {
12	  header: AcceptHeader
13	  supports: string[]
14	  default: string
15	}
16	
17	export interface acceptsOptions extends acceptsConfig {
18	  match?: (accepts: Accept[], config: acceptsConfig) => string
19	}
20	
21	export const defaultMatch = (accepts: Accept[], config: acceptsConfig): string => {
22	  const { supports, default: defaultSupport } = config
23	  const accept = accepts.sort((a, b) => b.q - a.q).find((accept) => supports.includes(accept.type))
24	  return accept ? accept.type : defaultSupport
25	}
26	
27	/**
28	 * Match the accept header with the given options.
29	 * @example
30	 * ```ts
31	 * app.get('/users', (c) => {
32	 *   const lang = accepts(c, {
33	 *     header: 'Accept-Language',
34	 *     supports: ['en', 'zh'],
35	 *     default: 'en',
36	 *   })
37	 * })
38	 * ```
39	 */
40	export const accepts = (c: Context, options: acceptsOptions): string => {
41	  const acceptHeader = c.req.header(options.header)
42	  if (!acceptHeader) {
43	    return options.default
44	  }
45	  const accepts = parseAccept(acceptHeader)
46	  const match = options.match || defaultMatch
47	
48	  return match(accepts, options)
49	}
